channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nikola Jokic acknowledges Denver’s struggles after playoff exit, reiterates commitment to Nuggets
item.description: Nikola Jokic expressed both disappointment in the Denver Nuggets’ early playoff exit and a strong desire to remain with the franchise long term following their Game 6 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolve...

item.title: Samia links future salary increase to workers’ performance and economic growth
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has said the government plans to increase workers’ salaries next year, not immediately, while calling on employees to improve their performance and professionalism. She m...

item.title: Brain imaging study identifies three ADHD subtypes, including one linked to severe emotional dysregulation
item.description: New research using brain imaging suggests that attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) may not be a single, uniform condition but instead consists of at least three distinct subtypes. Traditio...

item.title: Two new wild poliovirus cases confirmed in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan's 2026 polio tally rises to three
item.description: Pakistan has reported two fresh cases of wild poliovirus from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, increasing the total number of polio cases for the year 2026 to three. According to an official from the Paki...

item.title: INEC Announces June 20, 2026 for By-elections in Enugu, Nasarawa, Rivers, Ondo, Kebbi and Kano
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) under Chairman Professor Joash Amupitan has announced that by-elections go dey hold on 20th June 2026 for six different states across Nigeria. This...

item.title: Fabio Wardley to defend WBO heavyweight title against Daniel Dubois on 9 May in Manchester
item.description: Fabio Wardley and Daniel Dubois will face each other in a significant all-British heavyweight clash on Saturday 9 May at the Co-op Live arena in Manchester. Wardley, the unbeaten 31-year-old from Ipsw...

item.title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 Sees Strong Adoption in India With Modest Global Growth
item.description: OpenAI's ChatGPT Images 2.0, launched last week, has quickly become popular in India, emerging as the largest user base for the new image-generation feature. The upgrade allows for more complex prompt...

item.title: Russia Successfully Conducts Suborbital Test Flight of New Soyuz-5 Rocket Powered by RD-171MV Engine
item.description: On April 30, 2026, Russia achieved a significant milestone in its space program with the successful suborbital test launch of the first Soyuz-5 rocket from Baikonur Cosmodrome's PU-1 pad in the Baiter...

item.title: Australia's Q1 Inflation Rises to Highest Level in Over Two Years, Raising Prospects of Further RBA Rate Hikes
item.description: Australia's consumer price inflation climbed to 4.09% year-over-year in the first quarter of 2026, marking the highest level since the fourth quarter of 2023. Although this figure came in below econom...

item.title: eThekwini and Mangaung Top South Africa's Water Loss Rankings with Over 40% Leakage
item.description: According to data obtained from South Africa's Department of Water and Sanitation, eThekwini (Durban) and Mangaung (Bloemfontein) have the highest rates of water loss among major cities in the country...

item.title: The Role of Amateurs, Activists, and Hackers in the Development and Democratization of Science
item.description: The article explores the often-overlooked contributions of non-professional participants to the advancement of science throughout history. It argues that while society owes much to scientific progress...

item.title: Hyderabad's New Food Offerings: Burmese Festival at Mekong and Fresh Dining Spots
item.description: This article from The Hindu highlights the latest food experiences available in Hyderabad for the weekend. It focuses on new menus that bring out regional flavours, month-long food festivals, and fres...

item.title: Armed groups as governance providers in Mali: why military solutions alone no fit end the crisis
item.description: Mali don dey inside political turmoil since 2012 wey military coup happen and armed groups take over northern regions. Efforts to set up transitional governments don fail, and by May 2025 the military...

item.title: Dance Scenes and Ritual in Zimbabwean Rock Art: Insights from Archaeological and Ethnographic Analysis
item.description: Researchers from the ERC Artsoundscapes project have been studying rock paintings in Zimbabwe, which were created over the last 10,000 years by hunter-gatherer groups and later farming communities. Th...

item.title: WHO chief says US has outstanding financial obligations despite planned exit
item.description: Ayon sa ulat, sinabi ng pinuno ng World Health Organization (WHO) na may natitira pang obligasyong pinansyal ang Estados Unidos kahit ito ay nasa proseso ng pag-alis o pagbabawas ng ugnayan sa organis...

item.title: Trump maintains blockade on Iran as tensions over Strait of Hormuz escalate and Putin suggests diplomatic path
item.description: US President Donald Trump has refused to lift a naval blockade on Iran’s ports, insisting that sanctions and military pressure will remain in place until Tehran agrees to a deal addressing its nuclear...

item.title: Trucking industry says most carriers won’t change speeds during Alberta’s QEII 120 km/h pilot
item.description: A pilot project raising the speed limit to 120 km/h on Alberta’s Queen Elizabeth II Highway (QEII) is not expected to significantly change how large trucking companies operate, according to the Albert...

item.title: CDC Reports Outbreak of Antibiotic-Resistant Salmonella Linked to Backyard Chickens in 13 States
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has issued a warning about an ongoing outbreak of antibiotic-resistant Salmonella bacteria connected to backyard poultry across the United States. ...

item.title: Tanzania obtains $1.27 billion financing to advance central SGR railway expansion
item.description: Tanzania has secured a major financing package worth $1.277 billion (about Sh3.3 trillion) to continue construction of its Standard Gauge Railway (SGR), specifically covering the Makutupora to Isaka s...

item.title: Lanzerac Wine Estate challenges SARB forfeiture order over R220m property linked to Markus Jooste
item.description: The luxury Lanzerac Wine Estate in Stellenbosch, previously associated with the late controversial businessman Markus Jooste, is taking legal action against the South African Reserve Bank (SARB). The ...
